segments.text: Job further continued his discourse, and said:
Oh, that I were as in months past, As in the days when God watched over me;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When His lamp shone upon my head, And when by His light I walked through darkness;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Just as I was in the days of my prime, When the friendly counsel of God was over my tent;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When the Almighty was yet with me, When my children were around me;
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When my steps were bathed with cream, And the rock poured out rivers of oil for me!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I went out to the gate by the city, When I took my seat in the open square,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The young men saw me and hid, And the aged arose and stood;
The princes refrained from talking, And put their hand on their mouth;
The voice of nobles was hushed, And their tongue stuck to the roof of their mouth.
When the ear heard, then it blessed me, And when the eye saw, then it approved me;
Because I delivered the poor who cried out, The fatherless and the one who had no helper.
The blessing of a perishing man came upon me, And I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.
I put on righteousness, and it clothed me; My justice was like a robe and a turban.
I was eyes to the blind, And I was feet to the lame.
I was a father to the poor, And I searched out the case that I did not know.
I broke the fangs of the wicked, And plucked the victim from his teeth.
Then I said, 'I shall die in my nest, And multiply my days as the sand.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My root is spread out to the waters, And the dew lies all night on my branch.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My glory is fresh within me, And my bow is renewed in my hand.'
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Men listened to me and waited, And kept silence for my counsel.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: After my words they did not speak again, And my speech settled on them as dew.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They waited for me as for the rain, And they opened their mouth wide as for the spring rain.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I mocked at them, they did not believe it, And the light of my countenance they did not cast down.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I chose the way for them, and sat as chief; So I dwelt as a king in the army, As one who comforts mourners.
segments.speaker: Job
